target.name: Slate Auto
target.founder: Miles Arnone (Co-founder, CEO of Re:Build Manufacturing), Will Barker (Co-founder, Re:Build), Jeff Wilke (Co-founder, ex-CEO Worldwide Consumer at Amazon)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.4B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Slate Auto is a Series C transportation company founded by Miles Arnone, Will Barker, and Jeff Wilke (former CEO of Worldwide Consumer at Amazon). The company has raised $1.4B to date. The transportation sector peer universe consists of seven private companies, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, ZeroAvia, and Harbinger, with no listed public comparables.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-stage advancement. Using total capital raised ($1.4B) as a proxy for post-money valuation, this method yields an implied valuation of $2.4B. A sensitivity range incorporating conservative and optimistic applications of this multiplier produces a low estimate of $2.0B and a high estimate of $2.7B.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow transportation peer set. While seven private transportation companies exist, granular stage and valuation-tier filtering yields fewer than the three minimum datapoints required for robust median benchmarking. The single-method triangulation supports a median valuation of $2.4B with a range of $2.0B to $2.7B. Key uncertainties include the stability of stage-progression multiples in frontier transportation, the relevance of the wider peer universe to Slate Auto's specific subsector positioning, and forward capital requirements.
